objectives: Write clean Python code for data and model pipelines
Use Python to structure reusable functions for preprocessing and evaluation
Prepare Python environments and scripts for repeatable experimentation
learning_path.title: Python Foundations for ML Workflows

objectives: Query and filter data needed for model training
Join multiple tables to create training datasets
Validate dataset sizes and basic integrity using SQL queries
learning_path.title: SQL for Data Extraction and ML Dataset Building

objectives: Handle missing values, outliers, and inconsistent formats
Encode categorical variables and scale/normalize numeric features
Create train/validation/test splits and prevent data leakage
learning_path.title: Data Preprocessing and Feature Engineering Essentials

objectives: Select appropriate metrics for classification and regression
Compute and interpret confusion-matrix-derived metrics
Design evaluation protocols and compare models consistently
learning_path.title: Model Evaluation and Metrics for Reliable Comparison

objectives: Explain core deep learning concepts (layers, activations, loss, optimization)
Understand overfitting/underfitting and regularization strategies
Plan experiments and interpret training/validation curves
learning_path.title: Deep Learning Fundamentals and Training Dynamics

objectives: Implement neural network models and training loops in TensorFlow
Configure losses, optimizers, and callbacks for training control
Evaluate trained models using consistent metrics and validation splits
learning_path.title: TensorFlow: Building and Training Neural Networks

objectives: Build neural networks and training loops in PyTorch
Use datasets and dataloaders to feed training and validation data
Evaluate and compare model variants using consistent metrics
learning_path.title: PyTorch: Model Development and Experimentation

objectives: Design CI/CD steps for training, testing, and releasing models
Deploy models and manage versioning and rollbacks
Set up monitoring for data drift, performance, and operational health
learning_path.title: MLOps: CI/CD, Deployment, and Monitoring for ML Systems

matched_occupation.description: Computer vision engineers research, design, develop, and train artificial intelligence algorithms and machine learning primitives that understand the content of digital images based on a large amount of data. They apply this understanding to solve different real-world problems such as security, autonomous driving, robotic manufacturing, digital image classification, medical image processing and diagnosis, etc.
matched_occupation.label: computer vision engineer
